data.title: Dizal to Report Key Progress in its NSCLC Portfolio with Two Oral Presentations and a Poster During ASCO 2026
data.description: The groundbreaking Phase 3 clinical trial known as WU-KONG28, examining the effectiveness of the medication ZEGFROVY® in treating EGFR mutated NSCLC following treatment with Tagrisso and other third-generation EGFR TKIs, has been chosen to be presented as a Late-Breaking Abstract Oral at DZD6008. DZD6008 is a fourth-generation EGFR TKI. Additionally, a poster presentation will highlight the clinical advantages of golidocitinib in the same setting. This study showcases the potential of these medications in addressing an unmet medical need and advancing treatment options for patients with these specific mutations.

data.title: Los Angeles Private Investigator Warns Smart Home Devices Are Being Used for Surveillance in Divorce, Stalking, and Corporate Disputes
data.description: A private investigator from Los Angeles has raised concerns about the growing use of smart home devices for covert surveillance in various real-life situations, such as divorce proceedings, stalking incidents, and business disputes. According to Empire Pacific Investigative Services, there has been a significant increase in the number of cases involving the misuse of these devices for spying purposes.

data.title: America's Largest Homebuilder D.R. Horton Says Affordability Is Hitting Housing Demand
data.description: D.R. Horton, Inc. (NYSE: DHI) saw an increase in shares following the release of their fiscal second-quarter 2026 results. The American home construction company, headquartered in Arlington, Texas, reported a net income of $647.9 million, or $2.24 per diluted share, which was down from $810.4 million, or $2.58 per share, compared to the previous year. Despite the decline in revenue to $7.558 billion from $7.734 billion, earnings per share surpassed analyst expectations at $2.17. The company, the largest homebuilder by volume in the US since 2002, achieved a consolidated pre-tax income of $867.4 million, with a pre-tax profit margin of 11.5%. This was aided by a 40 basis point increase in margins due to lower warranty costs and a favorable litigation outcome. Homebuilding revenue decreased by 2% to $7.1 billion, with homes closed seeing a 1% increase.

data.title: Five Hoxhunt Customers Win Prestigious CSO Awards for Innovation in Human Risk Management
data.description: Today, Hoxhunt, a premier Human Risk Management platform, acknowledged Uber, Monster Energy, Docusign, LyondellBasell, and Copart for their exceptional programs supported and expanded with Hoxhunt. This recognition was made on April 21, 2026, in Minneapolis. The companies' achievements have garnered attention for their award-winning approaches.

data.title: Groundwater Festival Comes to Life in Lakewood on May 2, 2026
data.description: Community members in LAKEWOOD, Calif. have the opportunity to enjoy a day of entertainment, raffles, and family activities at the upcoming 16th Annual Groundwater Festival hosted by the Water Replenishment District (WRD). The event is scheduled for Saturday, May 2, 2026, running from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. This free celebration aims to raise awareness about groundwater, with various live performances and fun-filled attractions planned.

data.title: ROHM Develops 5th Generation SiC MOSFETs with Approximately 30% Lower On-Resistance at High Temperatures
data.description: On April 21, 2026, ROHM Semiconductor unveiled the newest addition to its EcoSiC™ series in Santa Clara, CA, and Kyoto, Japan. The company introduced the 5th Generation SiC MOSFETs designed for high-efficiency power applications. This innovation is specifically tailored for automotive electric powertrain systems, including traction inverters for electric vehicles (xEVs), in addition to power supplies for AI servers and industrial machinery like data centers. These MOSFETs are optimized to enhance performance in these sectors.

data.title: Illinois Construction Hike for Hope Brings Illinois Communities Together to Confront Suicide Prevention
data.description: On June 13, the Hike for Hope Duel-Site Event is set to take place in Downers Grove and Grafton, Illinois. This event aims to mobilize the community in both locations to address the issue of suicide risk. The construction industry in the U.S. has one of the highest suicide rates, highlighting the urgent need for action. This event is scheduled for April 21, 2026, and seeks to raise awareness and provide support to combat this concerning trend. The event is crucial in tackling the challenges faced by the construction sector, which is marked by long hours and demanding work conditions.

data.title: Q1 2026 revenue of 27.6 m€: another consecutive quarter of sequential growth (+8%)
data.description: Paris, France - April 21, 2026 - EKINOPS, a prominent provider of optical networks, connectivity, and SASE cybersecurity solutions for Service Providers and enterprises, has disclosed its revenue for the first quarter of 2026 (January 1 to March 31, 2026). The revenue stood at m€28.5, as per IFRS Non-audited data. A comparison with the first quarter of 2025 shows a notable change, with an increase in revenue. Additionally, there was also a change observed when comparing the first quarter of 2026 with the fourth quarter of 2025. For further details, the complete story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Fake Iranian Officials Target Stranded Ships With Bitcoin Scam In Strait Of Hormuz
data.description: A warning was issued by Greek maritime risk management firm MARISKS on Monday regarding fraudsters impersonating Iranian officials targeting shipping companies with vessels stranded in the Strait of Hormuz. The scammers request Bitcoin (BTC) and Tether (USDT) in exchange for vessel ownership documents, cargo manifests, and crew lists. After analyzing the paperwork, they demand payments ranging from hundreds of thousands to millions of dollars in cryptocurrency. The fraudulent messages claim that once the documents are submitted and eligibility assessed by Iranian Security Services, a fee in cryptocurrency must be paid for the vessel to safely transit the strait. MARISKS emphasized that these messages are scams and are not associated with Iranian authorities. In a separate incident, one ship paid the demanded amount but was subsequently fired upon. More details are available on Benzinga.com.
